title: International Trade and Investment
text:
International Trade and Investment

The International Trade and Investment Program examines the causes and consequences of trade between and among nations and regions. It considers how transportation costs, tariffs, and other factors affect geographic specialization and commercial flows.
Read summaries of presentations at the latest program meeting
Read the latest Program Report
Affiliated scholars

Program Director

Stephen J. Redding is a Professor at Stanford University. His research interests span international trade, economic geography, and the determinants of productivity and economic growth. He has been an NBER affiliate since 2011.
